editor_settings.title: Before You Buy: Is the Canon G7X a DSLR Replacement?
editor_settings.content:
1. Defining the Terms

Many beginners refer to any "nice" camera that isn't a phone as a DSLR. The Canon G7X is not a DSLR (Digital Single-Lens Reflex). A DSLR utilizes internal mirrors to reflect light up into an optical viewfinder. The G7X is entirely digital with no internal mirrors, making it a compact camera.

2. Can It Replace a DSLR?

For 80% of normal consumers: Yes, it absolutely can. Most people buy entry-level DSLRs like the Canon Rebel series, leave the kit lens on permanently, and never change out the glass. In that scenario, the G7X actually produces drastically better images.

@rominagafur #photography

3. The Lens Superiority

A standard DSLR kit lens has an aperture of f/3.5 - f/5.6. This is terrible for dark rooms. The fixed lens permanently welded to the front of the G7X is an f/1.8 - f/2.8 lens. That means it lets in significantly more light than a beginner DSLR kit, resulting in much cleaner photos.


4. When You Need the Real Thing

If you want to take photos of birds in flight from a hundred yards away, or macro shots of insects, the G7X will fail you. Those scenarios require a DSLR or Mirrorless system so you can attach massive telephoto or specialized macro lenses.

1. Defining the Terms

Many beginners refer to any "nice" camera that isn't a phone as a DSLR. The Canon G7X is not a DSLR (Digital Single-Lens Reflex). A DSLR utilizes internal mirrors to reflect light up into an optical viewfinder. The G7X is entirely digital with no internal mirrors, making it a compact camera.

2. Can It Replace a DSLR?

For 80% of normal consumers: Yes, it absolutely can. Most people buy entry-level DSLRs like the Canon Rebel series, leave the kit lens on permanently, and never change out the glass. In that scenario, the G7X actually produces drastically better images.

@rominagafur #photography

3. The Lens Superiority

A standard DSLR kit lens has an aperture of f/3.5 - f/5.6. This is terrible for dark rooms. The fixed lens permanently welded to the front of the G7X is an f/1.8 - f/2.8 lens. That means it lets in significantly more light than a beginner DSLR kit, resulting in much cleaner photos.


4. When You Need the Real Thing

If you want to take photos of birds in flight from a hundred yards away, or macro shots of insects, the G7X will fail you. Those scenarios require a DSLR or Mirrorless system so you can attach massive telephoto or specialized macro lenses.
editor_settings.author: Roderick Scott
editor_settings.meta_description: I see dozens of beginners asking if the Canon G7X is a good beginner DSLR. No, it isn't, and understanding why could save you hundreds of dollars.
